rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS BELL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Campbell Construction Co.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Campbell Construction Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff Douglas Bell does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) Defendant does not address Plaintiff's interrogatory responses identifying witnesses Pat Tillman and Robert Lee, or his deposition testimony identifying classmates Pat Tillman, Brenda and Tom Mock, Mel Friedman, and Lee Boek. (Defendant's Ex. B [Plaintiff's Supplemental/Amended Supplemental/Amended Responses to Standard Interrogatories] at 1-2; Plaintiff's Ex. A [Deposition Testimony of Douglas Bell] at 15-21; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [the court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.= (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE HUBER ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC's Motion for Summary Adjudication: Hearing Required. The Court did not receive courtesy copies of the moving papers as required under Local Rule 2.7(B). The hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
